work.title: Tafsir al-Qur'an al-'Azim (Tafsir Ibn Kathir), Arabic, unabridged
work.author: Isma'il ibn Umar ibn Kathir
work.digital_source: QUL resource 22 via spa5k/tafsir_api mirror, edition ar-tafsir-ibn-kathir

distributed.grades_note: grades not published here; per-surah fidelity in QUALITY.md, full grade cards retained by maintainer

independence.translator_input: public-domain Arabic only; no English translation ever provided
independence.grader_role: post-completion accuracy check vs the Arabic; a published scholar-reviewed English edition was consulted internally ONLY as a verification benchmark to detect AI hallucination (invented meanings, garbled rulings) — comparing meanings, not wording; never an input to the translation
independence.leak_filter: redactReferenceLeaks — strips reference wording from grader output
legal_basis_summary: Independent creation: infringement requires copying; the translator never had access (Feist v. Rural, 499 U.S. 340)
Copyright protects expression, not meanings/ideas/facts (17 U.S.C. 102(b)); the benchmark comparison was of meanings

Internal non-expressive analysis of entire copyrighted works held transformative fair use (Authors Guild v. Google, 804 F.3d 202 (2d Cir. 2015); Authors Guild v. HathiTrust, 755 F.3d 87 (2d Cir. 2014); Bartz v. Anthropic (N.D. Cal. 2025))
Distinguished from Thomson Reuters v. Ross (D. Del. 2025): the reference was never an input to building the translation; the work was complete before any comparison
Underlying Arabic work is public domain; anyone may translate it

overlap_evidence.note: Residual overlap concentrates in unavoidable shared material (Qur'anic verse wording, proper names, isnad chains, standard honorific formulas), not expository prose.

disclaimer: Factual/technical provenance record, not legal advice. Have counsel review before relying on it.
